determinism_check.caveat: Baselines and avmp_static_mr05 were not re-run for cost reasons. The deterministic subset excludes latency and other timing-sensitive columns by design. The auto-trigger uses time.monotonic_ns to throttle (RFC 0002 section 4.2), so its decisions are wall-clock dependent; byte-identity is NOT expected for rebalance_enabled=True runs, and the rerun confirmed this. See RFC 0002 section 8 question 5.
determinism_check.method: Independent rerun (avmp_dynamic_mr05 variant), compared to primary aggregated_deterministic.json rows filtered to variant_label == avmp_dynamic_mr05
determinism_check.result: NOT byte-identical (primary SHA-256 aca213258f349d58140c2eb47c7de152f2536f18d6d5d715ccc8b3d0b77de04c; rerun SHA-256 26e3acedd92f06e9824bc0434f5bf4aaa78d488b8f51a8ce5f791908e0c033a1). Concrete instance of RFC 0002 section 8 question 5: auto-trigger ordering depends on time.monotonic_ns through the throttle interval, so the migration sequence varies between runs and propagates into per-cell aggregated counters.

success_criterion.criterion: AVMP v2 must achieve total_oom <= min(fixed_dual_mr05.total_oom, fixed_dual_mr09.total_oom) on every workload in the default 270-cell sweep, with the v2 variant configured_total_bytes not exceeding any baseline configured_total_bytes.
success_criterion.note: avmp_dynamic_mr05 cross-workload total exceeds the min-baseline (fixed_dual_mr05 = 784.7) by 4.6 OOMs. uniform_short cells never sustained pressure (rebalance_count = 0 for every avmp_dynamic uniform_short row) because the brief-specified placement (after successful allocate/free) does not reach OOM-prone code paths. mixed_long cells did trigger heavily (313 to 1531 migrations per cell) and produced near-parity OOM outcomes. Follow-up: move the auto-trigger into the CapacityError branch per RFC 0002 section 4.2 wording (which differs from the sub-PR 3 brief placement).
